evidence: The paper uses random forest classifiers (R/ranger v0.10.1) for brain tumor classification but does not report or interpret variable importance. "For each sample, an ad hoc random forest classifier...was trained using the Heidelberg reference cohort...and a final report containing sequencing statistics and classification results was generated." All conclusions focus on classification accuracy (concordance with neuropathology: 89%), out-of-bag error rates (mean 5.81%), and clinical impact on surgical decision-making. No feature importance measures or rankings are presented or discussed.
